records.ety: [F. dtritus, fr. L. detritus, p. p. of deterere. See Detriment.]

defs: A mass of substances worn off from solid bodies by attrition, and reduced to small portions; as, diluvial detritus.
notes: ☞ For large portions, the word dbris is used.

defs: Hence: Any fragments separated from the body to which they belonged; any product of disintegration.
quotes: The mass of detritus of which modern languages are composed.
